metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the veto insertion point: since `det_floor.py` produces DetResult objects before any G5 LLM findings exist, the child-count veto must be applied as a post-Pass-1 filter step in `run_pass1` or the orchestrator (e.g., after collecting all Pass-1 findings, drop any G5 findings tagged 'flat/undecomposed' when `len(ctx.children) > 0`). Update the plan's Scope/files to name the correct seam.
findings.finding: The plan names `det_floor.py` (or the DET-invariant seam) as the location for the G5 'flat/undecomposed' veto, but this is architecturally impossible: det_floor.py runs the static P1–P9 DET tier independently and BEFORE the LLM tier produces any G5 findings. run_det_floor() returns DetResult objects; Pass-1 then separately produces LLM findings via pass1_chunk. det_floor.py has no access to LLM-tier findings and cannot veto or drop them post-hoc. The correct seam must be after G5 findings are collected in run_pass1 or the orchestrator.
findings.location: Scope / files — 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py (or the DET-invariant seam)' / AC: 'A DET guard suppresses a G5 decomposition finding when the ticket has >=1 child'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the target file from `det_floor.py` to the post-Pass-1 filtering step in `run_pass1` (pass1.py) or a dedicated `_apply_det_veto(findings, ctx)` helper called after `findings.extend(fu.result())` in the ThreadPoolExecutor loop. The guard reads `ctx.children` (already available) and filters the collected LLM findings list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Scope / files section whether the G5 DET veto is a post-`run_pass1` filter in `pass1.py` (dropped before Pass-2) or in `orchestrator.py`'s `partition_findings`; remove the ambiguous `det_floor.py (or the DET-invariant seam)` phrasing for this hunk.
findings.finding: The plan says the DET veto should live in `det_floor.py` "(or the DET-invariant seam)" but a post-LLM finding filter that drops G5 results is not a DET floor check — `det_floor.run_det_floor` runs before Pass-1 and produces `DetResult` objects, not LLM finding dicts. The actual suppression of a G5 finding must happen after `run_pass1` returns, either in `pass1.py` (post-processing the findings list) or in `orchestrator.py`'s verdict assembly. Neither location is named.
findings.location: Scope / files section — `det_floor.py (or the DET-invariant seam)` named for the DET veto; AC line 2 — "A DET guard suppresses a G5 decomposition finding"
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'det_floor.py (or the DET-invariant seam)' with the actual seam: e.g. `pass1.py` — after `run_pass1` returns its findings list, filter out any finding whose `criteria` includes 'G5' and whose text signals 'flat/undecomposed' when `len(ctx.children) > 0`; or add a `_apply_det_vetoes(findings, ctx)` helper called from `run_pass1` before returning.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify exactly WHERE the DECOMPOSITION STATE block is injected: name the function (`pass1_chunk`, `_criterion_block`, a new per-criterion context hook, or the `instructions` string) and whether it is conditioned on `c['id'] == 'G5'` or appended to the system prompt for all criteria.
findings.finding: The plan says to inject the DECOMPOSITION STATE block 'into the G5 finder context from ctx.children' in pass1.py, but G5 is a 1-TURN criterion whose per-criterion instructions are assembled in passes.py:pass1_chunk via _criterion_block + a generic instructions string — there is no G5-specific context augmentation path in pass1.py, and no specification of WHERE in the call chain (system prompt, instructions string, or a new per-criterion hook) the block is inserted.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `context_extras` dict to `pass1_chunk` (keyed by criterion id) and append the block to the `instructions` string when `c['id'] == 'G5'`; specify this in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the post-LLM location for the G5 veto (e.g., a filter in `run_pass1` after findings are collected, or in `orchestrator.py` before Pass-2), and remove the reference to `det_floor.py` as the veto site.
findings.finding: The plan places the DET veto in 'det_floor.py (or the DET-invariant seam)', but det_floor.py runs entirely before Pass-1 LLM findings exist and has no access to them. A veto that drops a G5 finding must operate after pass1_chunk produces findings — the plan does not resolve this temporal contradiction.
findings.location: ## Scope / files — det_floor.py bullet; det_floor.py:run_det_floor (lines 785-821); pass1.py:run_pass1
findings.suggested_fix: Place the veto as a filter loop in `run_pass1` immediately after the ThreadPoolExecutor block (pass1.py ~line 456), operating on the assembled `findings` list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the G5 veto is a **post-LLM-findings filter** applied after `run_pass1` collects findings (e.g. a filter step inside `run_pass1` or the orchestrator), not a DET-floor check — update the file target accordingly (not `det_floor.py`).
findings.finding: The plan places the G5 veto in `det_floor.py` (or the DET-invariant seam), but those layers run BEFORE LLM-tier findings exist — G5 is a 1-TURN LLM criterion, so its findings only exist after `run_pass1` returns. The veto therefore cannot be a DET-floor check; it must be a post-collection filter applied after `pass1_chunk` returns findings (e.g. inside `run_pass1` or the orchestrator). Placing it in `det_floor.py` is a mechanism mislocation that will confuse the implementer.
findings.suggested_fix: Specify the veto as a post-collection filter step inside `run_pass1` in `pass1.py` (e.g., after the ThreadPoolExecutor results are aggregated): `findings = [f for f in findings if not (_is_g5_undecomposed(f) and ctx.has_children)]`. Rename the implementation target from `det_floor.py` to `pass1.py` (or a new `g5_veto` helper).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan that `pass1.py:427` is a coverage/observability record only, and explicitly specify HOW `ctx.children` will be threaded into the G5 finder prompt (e.g., as an additional parameter to `pass1_chunk`, a pre-processing step in `run_pass1`, or as a preamble injected into `plan` before the G5 call).
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'counted in pass1.py:427' as evidence that ctx.children is already authoritative for G5, but line 427 is a batch-plan observability record (coverage['batch_plan']['children']) — it is NOT part of the G5 finder's input. pass1_chunk receives only the raw plan text string; ctx.children is never threaded into any G5 prompt context. The plan's framing implies existing plumbing that does not exist, potentially leading the implementer to skip building the actual injection mechanism.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'counted in pass1.py:427' with a precise description of the new injection mechanism: e.g., 'a DECOMPOSITION STATE block will be prepended to the plan text passed to pass1_chunk when G5 is in the chunk, derived from ctx.children'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the discrimination mechanism for the veto: either (a) have the model tag the G5 `checklist_key` (`single_concern` vs. `leaf_coherent`/`yagni`) in its output and veto only `single_concern` findings when children > 0, or (b) explicitly scope the veto to ALL G5 findings when `has_children` is True and remove the 'G5 still judges child-content/altitude' carve-out (since G3/G4 cover child quality for containers).
findings.finding: The plan says the DET guard suppresses G5 findings about being 'flat/undecomposed' while still allowing G5 to 'judge child-content/altitude', but all G5 findings are free-text LLM output with no structured sub-type field. The plan does not specify how the veto discriminates a 'flat/undecomposed' finding from a 'child altitude' finding — leaving the implementer to either pattern-match finding prose (fragile) or use a blanket suppression of ALL G5 findings when children > 0 (which would wrongly drop legitimate child-quality findings).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'A DET guard suppresses a G5 decomposition finding when the ticket has >=1 child…; G5 still judges child-content/altitude.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the discrimination rule for the G5 veto: specify whether it drops ALL G5 findings when `len(ctx.children) > 0` (i.e., the full G5 criterion is bypassed for containers), or only a named sub-concern — and if the latter, specify the field/key/pattern used to identify it.
findings.finding: The veto decision rule 'drop any G5 flat/undecomposed finding' requires discriminating flat/undecomposed G5 findings from valid G5 findings about child-content/altitude, but the plan defines no field, key, substring pattern, or schema attribute that marks a finding as 'undecomposed' vs. 'child-content/altitude'. The P1Finding schema (finding, criteria, location, evidence, scenarios, impact, checklist_item, suggested_fix) has no such field.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify the cross-tier DET-veto pattern vs. the existing prior art of injecting store-derived context into the finder's `instructions` (as `_ticket_graph_blob` does for ISF in pass1.py:281-302), and specify whether the veto is a pre-call routing suppression or a post-call finding filter.
findings.finding: The plan proposes a novel cross-tier DET-veto pattern (DET floor suppresses an LLM-tier G5 finding) without considering the directly applicable prior art already in the codebase: `_ticket_graph_blob()` (pass1.py:281-302) injects pre-resolved store data into the ISF finder's `instructions`, which is the established approach for making store-derived facts authoritative in a single-turn LLM call. The same seam could inject the child-count block into G5's `pass1_chunk` call without a cross-tier veto mechanism, and the plan provides no justification for why the veto pattern is preferable.
findings.location: Scope / files — det_floor.py (or the DET-invariant seam)
findings.suggested_fix: Consider following the ISF prior art: inject a `DECOMPOSITION STATE` block into the G5 chunk's `instructions` in `pass1_chunk` (already threaded through `run_pass1` → `_chunk`), and strengthen the G5 rubric's checklist to treat store-confirmed children as authoritative. If a post-call veto is still preferred, name the specific pipeline location (e.g., between `run_pass1` return and Pass-2 dispatch in `orchestrator.py`) and explain why prompt injection is insufficient.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `passes.py` (specifically `pass1_chunk` / `_resolve_system` / the `plan-review-finder` prompt template) to the Scope / files section, since the G5 child-summary injection must thread through these seams, not just `pass1.py`.
findings.finding: `passes.py` (specifically `pass1_chunk` and `_resolve_system`) is the actual seam where G5 finder context is assembled and injected into the LLM call, but the plan names only `pass1.py` as the edit target. The `plan` string received by `pass1_chunk` is `ctx.plan_text` (title + description only); injecting the child summary into the G5 finder requires either augmenting `plan` before it reaches `pass1_chunk`, adding a new parameter to `pass1_chunk`/`pass1_with_ladder`, or modifying the prompt template — none of which is in `passes.py` as named.
findings.location: Scope / files section — `pass1.py` named as sole edit target for context injection
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope / files section, add: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py` — extend `pass1_chunk` (or `_resolve_system`) to accept an optional `context_addendum: str` that is prepended to the system prompt for G5-chunk calls when `ctx.has_children`; or augment `plan` in `pass1.py:_chunk` for G5-containing chunks before calling `_pass1_with_ladder`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for AC1 that directly asserts the constructed G5 finder context string contains the `DECOMPOSITION STATE` block with correct child data (e.g., a unit test that inspects the prompt/context built by `pass1.py` for a fixture ticket with known children).
findings.finding: AC1 ('The G5 finder context includes the store-sourced authoritative child summary') has no concrete proving command; the regression test in AC3 proves the end-state output but does not directly verify that the context block was correctly formatted and injected.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a unit test in `tests/unit/` that constructs a `ctx` with synthetic children, calls the G5 context-builder, and asserts the resulting string contains `DECOMPOSITION STATE (from store): N direct children:` with the correct aliases/titles — separate from the end-to-end regression test in AC3.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback behaviour when `ctx.children` is empty and the veto cannot confirm child existence: document whether the veto fires conservatively (no suppression when count is uncertain) or adopts the established fail-open/abstain pattern.
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for the DET guard when ctx.children is unexpectedly empty due to a partial or failed N+1 read — the plan grants the store-derived count full authority with no degradation path, so a transient read error would cause the veto to silently fail to fire on a ticket that does have children.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC bullet 2; orchestrator.py:166-182 (N+1 read)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit to a single file location for the DET veto (e.g., state 'add to `det_floor.py`' or name the actual seam file) rather than offering an uninvestigated alternative.
findings.finding: The scope bullet names `det_floor.py (or the DET-invariant seam)` without committing to a specific location, deferring the implementation site decision to the executor.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `det_floor.py (or the DET-invariant seam)` with the definitive file path. If `det_floor.py` is correct, remove the parenthetical. If the seam is elsewhere, name it explicitly (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/g5_finder.py` post-model hook).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Standardize terminology: pick one name ('DET veto' or 'DET guard') and use it consistently across the Scope description and all AC lines.
findings.finding: The plan uses three different terms for the same mechanism: 'DET veto' (Scope section), 'DET guard' (AC2), and 'DET-invariant seam' (Scope parenthetical). An implementer verifying AC2 ('A DET guard suppresses...') may be uncertain whether this refers to the same artifact as the 'DET veto' described in the Scope section or a distinct construct.
findings.location: Scope/files paragraph (det_floor.py bullet) and Acceptance Criteria AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'DET guard' in AC2 with 'DET veto' to match the Scope section wording, or vice versa — choose one term and apply it throughout.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief note (CLAUDE.md entry, inline module docstring, or ADR) documenting the store-grounded deterministic veto pattern in det_floor.py — what it does, why it lives there, and the contract that store-derived child count is authoritative over model output for G5.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural pattern — a deterministic, store-grounded veto in det_floor.py that overrides LLM output — but does not call for any documentation update (ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc) to capture this decision.
findings.location: Scope / files — det_floor.py (DET veto); AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a module-level docstring to det_floor.py explaining the pattern, and add one line to CLAUDE.md (or equivalent design doc) noting that det_floor vetoes are the canonical place to enforce store-derived invariants over LLM findings.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the DET veto guards against `ctx.children` being None or absent (e.g., `len(ctx.children or []) > 0`), and define the fallback behavior (pass through to LLM judgment) when child data is unavailable.
findings.finding: The DET veto (`len(ctx.children) > 0`) will raise a TypeError if `ctx.children` is None (e.g., if the upstream N+1 read in orchestrator.py failed silently or the attribute was never set), and the plan specifies no defensive handling or fallback for this case.
findings.location: det_floor.py / DET veto AC: 'A DET guard suppresses a G5 decomposition finding when the ticket has >=1 child (count from the store, not the model)'
findings.suggested_fix: In det_floor.py: `children = getattr(ctx, 'children', None) or []; if len(children) > 0: suppress G5 flat/undecomposed finding`. Document that None/missing children falls through to model judgment rather than crashing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability requirement: the DET veto must emit a debug-level log entry (including ticket ID and child count) whenever it suppresses a G5 finding.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a deterministic veto that silently suppresses G5 findings but specifies no observability (log, metric, or trace) for when the veto fires, making it impossible for operators to distinguish 'G5 suppressed by DET veto' from 'G5 never triggered'.
findings.suggested_fix: In det_floor.py, when suppressing: `logger.debug('G5 flat/undecomposed suppressed by DET veto: ticket=%s children=%d', ctx.ticket_id, len(children))`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate and acknowledge downstream consumers of G5 findings (orchestrator, CI gate, reporting) in the plan, and confirm the DET veto's change to G5 fire-rate is acceptable to each.
findings.finding: The plan changes the observable output of the G5 finder (a previously-firing finding is now suppressed by a new DET veto) but does not acknowledge any downstream caller — orchestrator, CI gate, reporting pipeline, or any other consumer — that may depend on the current G5 output shape or firing frequency.
findings.location: Scope / files — det_floor.py DET veto; AC line 2 ('DET guard suppresses a G5 decomposition finding')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Caller impact' section listing every consumer of plan-review G5 findings (e.g. orchestrator result aggregation, CI gate threshold, any dashboard) and explicitly state that suppressing G5 'undecomposed' findings for tickets with children is the desired new contract for each.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale in the plan explaining why BOTH the context injection AND the deterministic veto are needed together (e.g. injection reduces model error; veto provides a model-compliance-independent safety net).
findings.finding: The plan states the chosen approach (inject authoritative child summary + DET veto) but does not provide a positive rationale for WHY this two-component approach is preferred over injection-alone — i.e. it doesn't articulate that the veto provides a deterministic safety net independent of model compliance, which is the key fitness argument for the combination.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the ## What section: 'The context injection makes the model's task accurate; the deterministic veto provides a model-compliance-independent backstop — together they make the fix robust to both model hallucination and prompt-noncompliance.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
